page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-80 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, alongside 320 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the n4-standard-80 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score of 64099.5 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 18348.0. Its single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1773.0. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, delivering a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 4980.71 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds of 123.18 GB/sec. The instance excels in static web serving throughput, reaching a top-tier 95.08 GB/sec, and shows strong capabilities in image processing and database operations.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads that can leverage its 80 dedicated vCPUs and advanced instruction sets without requiring local NVMe storage or discrete GPU acceleration. The inclusion of AMX and AVX-512 allows the CPU to handle workloads like medium-scale LLM inference efficiently, processing 263.93 tokens per second on a 7B model. This server is highly suited for enterprise web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-80 is a general-purpose instance configured with 80 dedicated vCPUs and 320 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 40 physical cores, it features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks highlight strong multi-core capabilities, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 64099.5 and top-tier static web serving throughput of 95.08 GB/sec. Memory performance is robust, with cached read bandwidth reaching 4980.71 GB/sec. The n4-standard-80 is highly efficient for parallelized workloads, making it well-suited for enterprise web serving, database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-80 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 80 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and advanced AMX/AVX-512 instructions, delivering strong multi-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-80 is a general-purpose instance featuring 80 Intel vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual machine featuring eighty dedicated vCPUs, advanced matrix extensions, and exceptional multi-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 320 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong multi-core performance with top-tier static web serving throughput of 95.08 GB/sec
High-bandwidth memory subsystem delivering up to 4980.71 GB/sec cached read speeds
Optimized for enterprise web serving, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference
